reasoning: The question asks whether there will be a >10x spike in 'Explosions/Remote violence' in Thailand for the 30 days preceding the resolution dates, compared to `1 + 30-day average of the past 360 days`. The prompt states the reference value is 6.333, which means the 30-day average has been 5.333, and the threshold to resolve YES is 63.33 (so 64 or more events in a single month). Historically, Thailand sees about 5 explosion/remote violence events per month. The previous year had roughly 64 such events in total (5.333 * 12). Resolving YES would require a single month to witness as many explosions as an entire typical year. While the Deep South insurgency occasionally stages coordinated overnight attacks (such as 17 bombs in Aug 2022 and 40 arson/bomb attacks in March 2024), hitting 64+ explosion events specifically within one month is exceptionally rare and would mark an unprecedented escalation in the conflict. Furthermore, there have been no recent reports of 64+ bombings in June 2025, heavily suppressing the probability for the initial resolution dates. Uncertainty increases marginally for dates further in the future, but the required massive anomaly keeps estimates very low overall.
